preview.summary: Group G favourites Belgium take on a stubborn Iran side in a contrast of styles at SoFi. The Red Devils, unbeaten in qualifying and stacked with Premier League and elite-club talent, will dominate possession and probe for openings, while Iran sit deep, stay compact and look to spring on the counter. For Iran, frustrating one of the group's heavyweights would be a major step toward the knockouts.
preview.homeForm: Confirmed their place with an unbeaten qualifying run under Rudi Garcia; among the world's top ten.
preview.awayForm: Qualified comfortably from AFC and built around a well-drilled defensive block; ranked around 21st.
preview.homeKey: Jeremy Doku, electric dribbler who can change games instantly
preview.awayKey: Mehdi Taremi, Iran's captain and focal point in attack
storylines: Possibly Kevin De Bruyne's final World Cup
Belgium's firepower against Iran's disciplined low block
Lukaku included despite an injury-hit, low-scoring club season
preview.h2h: The two nations have never met; this is their first-ever fixture.
